title: Use Voice Mode with Hermes
summary: Practical guide for setting up and using all three Hermes voice modes: CLI microphone loop, voice replies in Telegram/Discord messaging, and Discord live voice channel bot, with provider recommendations, config tuning, and troubleshooting.

cli_commands.purpose: Install NeuTTS for local on-device TTS

cli_commands.command: brew install portaudio ffmpeg opus espeak-ng
sudo apt install portaudio19-dev ffmpeg libopus0 espeak-ng

config_keys.description: Silence detection sensitivity — higher = less sensitive
Seconds of silence before stopping recording
STT provider: local | groq | openai | mistral | xai
TTS provider: edge | elevenlabs | openai | mistral | neutts | xai

code_examples.caption: Recommended starter voice config
code_examples.code:
voice:
  record_key: "ctrl+b"
  max_recording_seconds: 120
  auto_tts: false
  beep_enabled: true
  silence_threshold: 200
  silence_duration: 3.0

stt:
  provider: "local"
  local:
    model: "base"

tts:
  provider: "edge"
  edge:
    voice: "en-US-AriaNeural"

code_examples.caption: Local NeuTTS config
code_examples.code:
tts:
  provider: "neutts"
  neutts:
    ref_audio: ''
    ref_text: ''
    model: neuphonic/neutts-air-q4-gguf
    device: cpu

gotchas: Get text working first, then add voice — don't try to debug both at once
Discord VC mode requires Presence Intent, Server Members Intent, and Message Content Intent in Developer Portal
Whisper garbage output usually means noisy environment, too-high silence_threshold, or wrong model size
Without portaudio, CLI microphone mode fails with 'No audio device found'
ffmpeg is required for Edge TTS audio conversion on messaging platforms
Bot in server channels typically requires @mention unless configured otherwise — DMs work without mention
